body.title: Provenance service: source trust scoring in Librarian recall
body.result: trust.py defines tier derivation from source_agent; recall() gains trust_tier field and min_trust_tier filter; SQL-aware mock fixes test ordering bug from WordGraph psycopg2 injection
body.description:
Librarian recall currently ranks results by FTS score + tag overlap + vector similarity. Add a fourth signal: source trust. Memories written by verified agents during human-checkpointed sessions score higher than memories written autonomously with no human review.

Trust tiers (v1, simple):
- tier_1: written by CC during an approved sprint (human in loop)
- tier_2: written by Igor during a human-checkpointed NE cycle
- tier_3: written autonomously with no checkpoint
- tier_0: no source_token (legacy / unknown origin)

Trust tier is derived from source_token metadata at query time — not stored on the memory itself (avoids stale trust if tier changes). Recall result includes trust_tier field so callers can filter or weight.

**Affected files:** devices/librarian/recall.py, devices/librarian/trust.py (new)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; trust scoring is additive to existing recall ranking, not a replacement
**Scope boundary:** IN — trust tier derivation from source_token; trust_tier in recall results; optional min_trust_tier filter param; OUT — trust decay over time, cross-agent trust delegation
**Completion criteria:** recall() returns results with trust_tier field; recall(topic='x', min_trust_tier=2) filters out tier_3 and tier_0 results; existing recall behavior unchanged when min_trust_tier not specified
